pattern: Each rotation changes the weighted sum by a fixed amount, so a linear recurrence derives every F(k) from the previous one using only the total and the element rotating to the front.
hints: Recomputing the whole weighted sum for each of the n rotations is quadratic; look for how F(k) relates to F(k-1).
Rotating by one increments every term's weight by one (adding the array total) except the element that wraps to position zero, which loses its full weight.
With S = sum(nums), F(k) = F(k-1) + S - n * nums[n - k]; start from F(0) and track the running maximum.

edge_inputs.description: maximum occurs at a nonzero rotation
all equal values give the same F for every rotation

stress.description: random array within the value bounds so all rotations matter
stress.note: large n confirms the linear recurrence against the quadratic oracle on small sizes too
